title: How exactly did Toujis sister get hurt
display_title: How exactly did Toujis sister get hurt
wikitext:
[]: How exactly did Touji's sister get hurt?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Sep 08, 2010 1:52 pm I'm watching the fight with Sachiel, and I can't help but notice that none of the buildings Eva-01 smashed into were occupied ones. The shelters were all underground, iirc. How did Eva-01 somehow hurt her? If anything, it's more likely that Sachiel did it with a stray cross-blast.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 08, 2010 2:54 pm It's entirely possible it was Sachiel, and Toji's just mad at Shinji for being unable to prevent it.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Sep 08, 2010 3:08 pm There are so many ways to get put into a hospital during an epic natural disaster scenario. Maybe the ground was shaking and she fell over. Maybe the Eva fucked up some infrastructure which caused some kind of panic. Did the show ever specify how exactly she was hurt?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Sep 08, 2010 3:11 pm I think they said she was trapped under some rubble. I could be wrong though.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Wed Sep 08, 2010 3:16 pm child of Lilith wrote: I think they said she was trapped under some rubble. I could be wrong though.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Sep 08, 2010 3:27 pm Well, even a shelter only holds up so well when demigods are wrestling in the middle of Tokyo-whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 08, 2010 4:16 pm ep3 script wrote: KENSUKE: I suppose so. So where have you been, Toji? You were gone for so long. Did you get caught in the crossfire or something? TOJI (OFF): My kid sister did. She was crushed under a pile of debris. She survived, but she's been in the hospital ever since. Both my pop and gramps work at the lab, and there's no way they can leave their posts right now. So, if I ain't with her, she's all alone at the hospital. TOJI: Man, that robot's pilot was plain awful! I'm downright pissed off! What the hell's the point of our own guy wreckin' the city?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Sep 08, 2010 4:20 pm Explosions the size of Sachiel's self-destruction do tend to spread damage around. Then again, explosions don't usually blast straight up into crosses.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Sep 08, 2010 5:03 pm I like Nemzie-kun's explanation. That'd also explain why Toji isn't so keen on seeing the fighting the way Kensuke is.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Sep 09, 2010 12:38 am Something just occurred to me when I was looking over the dialogue NemZ posted from Episode Three. I realize this may not be the best place to ask about this, so I apologize if there's a more appropriate thread for my comments, but since it partially involves Touji's sister, here goes nothing. Touji mentions that both his father and grandfather are busy working in the lab. I can only assume he means one of Nerv's labs (perhaps as technicians somewhere in the Geofront), since I suspect the majority of Tokyo III's residents are probably on Nerv's payroll to some extent. Now I don't pretend to know anything about Japan's corporate culture or whether comprehensive employee benefit packages are the norm. However, if that were the case, wouldn't some type of medical insurance plan be a part of the standard contractual agreement, and wouldn't such coverage extend to immediate family members/dependents? Basically, what I'm getting at is that we eventually learn about how Nerv recruited Touji to become the Fourth Child and that he listed medical treatment for his little sister's injuries as the sole condition of his agreement to pilot Unit-03. So I'm forced to wonder whether Nerv (and its affiliated hospitals) was already legally obligated to do everything they could to help Touji's sister, but elected to deliberately withhold potentially life-saving medical treatments because, let's face it, when push comes to shove (which, as it turns out, is quite often) Nerv is a heartless organization whose corrupt CEO makes it a policy to trade the lives of kids on a regular basis toward the realization of his precious scenario. To all the Gendo apologists out there (and you know who you are), feel free to discuss amongst yourselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Sep 09, 2010 1:09 am BC Baron wrote: Now I don't pretend to know anything about Japan's corporate culture or whether comprehensive employee benefit packages are the norm. However, if that were the case, wouldn't some type of medical insurance plan be a part of the standard contractual agreement, and wouldn't such coverage extend to immediate family members/dependents?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Sep 09, 2010 3:39 am I would suggest that the considerations Toji asks for are not standard treatments (she's already getting that at whatever hospital she's in) but rather for the quality of care and cutting-edge treatments that the VIPs of Nerv get at Nerv Cranial rather than just being stuck in a civilian hospital on the surface. That said, I wouldn't put it past Nerv to intentionally lower the available quality of care she's already getting or to lie about her prognosis in order to put more pressure on Toji.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Sep 09, 2010 10:49 am BC Baron wrote: So I'm forced to wonder whether Nerv (and its affiliated hospitals) was already legally obligated to do everything they could to help Touji's sister, but elected to deliberately withhold potentially life-saving medical treatments ...

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Sep 09, 2010 11:44 am In the manga, he ran into a building the first time he piloted Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Sep 09, 2010 12:14 pm thewayneiac wrote: The problem with this theory is that they didn't know until Ep. 17 that Toji was gong to be the next pilot. We are explicitly told in Ep. 04 that no one has been selected yet.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Sep 09, 2010 12:53 pm I've got to think that Toji and Kensuke's near-fatal adventure in ep3 put them at the top of the pilot candidates list. Ken was quite likely disqualified on the first pass do to vision impairment and the high likelihood of him becoming a security concern, so of course Toji gets the nod.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 09, 2010 1:55 pm That and Kensuke is way too emotionally adjusted and overeager. ...uh oh. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Sep 09, 2010 3:17 pm BC Baron wrote: While the audience didn't know and neither did any of Nerv's subordinates/bridge bunnies, that doesn't necessarily mean the people at the top like Gendo, Fuyutsuki and Ritsuko hadn't already made their decision well in advance and were just waiting for another Eva to become available.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Sep 09, 2010 3:58 pm Sachi wrote: The series actually more heavily implies that the pilot decisions are very spontaneous and they just pick somebody. I think it's Misato that comments about how Toji was picked incredibly fast.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Sep 09, 2010 5:09 pm BC Baron wrote: Sorry, I just can't agree that any decison possibly affecting the outcome of Gendo's precious scenario (including pilot selection) is done spontaneously.

The shelters were all underground, iirc. How did Eva-01 somehow hurt her? If anything, it's more likely that Sachiel did it with a stray cross-blast.
